report_name: New South Wales Government Downgrades 'Significant Cyber Incident' at Treasury, Attributed to Insider Threat

threat_actors.name: Insider Threat at NSW Treasury
threat_actors.description: A trusted internal staff member at NSW Treasury who allegedly abused their legitimate access to transfer a large volume of confidential commercial and financial documents to an external server.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The insider threat actor used their legitimate staff credentials to access and exfiltrate confidential documents from NSW Treasury.

attack_patterns.name: Automated Exfiltration
attack_patterns.description: The transfer of a large number of documents to an external server suggests an automated or scripted process was used for data exfiltration.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: Confidential documents were transferred to an external server, which was likely a personal cloud storage account or a similar web service.

identities.name: NSW Government
identities.description: The government of New South Wales, which managed the response to the cyber incident at its treasury department and officially downgraded it after containment.

identities.name: NSW Treasury
identities.description: The government agency targeted by an insider threat, resulting in the exfiltration of a large volume of confidential commercial and financial documents.

identities.name: NSW Police
identities.description: The law enforcement agency that investigated the data breach, launching 'Strike Force Civic' and laying criminal charges against the staff member involved.

infrastructure.name: External Data Server
infrastructure.description: An external server, likely a personal cloud storage service, used by the insider to exfiltrate a large volume of confidential documents from NSW Treasury.

indicators.name: Anomalous High-Volume Data Transfer
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Large File Transfer via Common Utilities
id: 2b8a1c3d-f4e5-4b6c-8d9e-a0b1c2d3e4f5
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of common command-line utilities to copy a large number of files, which could indicate data staging or exfiltration by an insider threat. This is a generic detection for behavior similar to the NSW Treasury incident.
references:
  - https://www.nsw.gov.au/media-releases/significant-cyber-incident-downgraded
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-04
tags:
  - attack.t1020
  - attack.exfiltration
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative scripts.
  - Backup jobs.
level: low
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_robocopy:
    Image|endswith: '\robocopy.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' /E '
      - ' /S '
      - ' /MIR '
  selection_xcopy:
    Image|endswith: '\xcopy.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' /E '
      - ' /S '
  condition: 1 of selection_*
indicators.description: Detects users transferring an unusually large volume of files using command-line tools, which could indicate an insider threat exfiltrating data, as seen in the NSW Treasury incident.

indicators.name: Data Exfiltration to Consumer Cloud Storage
indicators.pattern:
title: Data Exfiltration to Consumer Cloud Storage
id: 9a8b7c6d-5e4f-3a2b-1c0d-9e8f7a6b5c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects network connections to common consumer cloud storage services, which could indicate data exfiltration to an unauthorized external location, as was suspected in the NSW Treasury insider incident.
references:
  - https://www.nsw.gov.au/media-releases/significant-cyber-incident-downgraded
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-04
tags:
  - attack.t1567
  - attack.exfiltration
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of cloud storage if not explicitly forbidden by corporate policy.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    cs-host|endswith:
      - '.dropbox.com'
      - '.drive.google.com'
      - '.onedrive.live.com'
      - '.mega.nz'
      - '.box.com'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network connections to common consumer cloud storage services, which could indicate data exfiltration to an unauthorized external location.

relationships.source_name: Data Exfiltration to Consumer Cloud Storage
